# UC-22.60.6 · Portable Maintenance Access Terminal (PMAT) / maintenance-laptop governance - DO-355A baseline + USB / removable-media control + malware-detection events with aircraft-uplink risk score

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Mechanics use a laptop to load software into the aircraft. We make sure that laptop is clean and up-to-date before any mechanic plugs it into a plane - so no virus gets in by accident.*

---

## Description

Reconciles the PMAT inventory against EDR / SCCM baseline reports, Defender Device Control USB events, malware-detection events, and the work-order PMAT-tail mapping. Surfaces any PMAT with active findings that has a planned aircraft-uplink within the next 24 hours; routes to the CAMO Manager and Director of Maintenance to block the work-order until the PMAT is cleared.

## Value

A compromised PMAT is one of the highest-likelihood attack paths to a modern aircraft - the threat condition is named explicitly in DO-326A §3.2 and is the basis of multiple FAA Special Conditions. Operator value: avoid an aircraft uplink with an infected laptop, avoid an emergency AD, avoid an EASA / FAA enforcement action under Part-IS / Part-145.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `do355a_pmat_inventory_lookup` from the maintenance organisation's IT asset register (asset class = `pmat` or `maintenance-laptop`). (2) Tag PMAT hosts in Microsoft Defender / CrowdStrike with `pmat_in_scope=true` so the SCCM baseline checks treat them as a separate device group with the PMAT-specific golden image. (3) Configure Defender Device Control with the operator's allowlisted USB hardware IDs (organisation-issued data-loaders, software-loaded USB hardware) and treat all other USB insertions as violations. (4) Schedule UC-22.60.6 every 15 minutes; route `risk_score >= 90` at severity P1, `risk_score 70-89` at severity P2. (5) Build the `PMAT Security Health` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.60.6` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-355A-s2-1"`. Every row pins (pmat_id, technician_id, last_baseline_check, baseline_drift_finding, usb_event_count, malware_event_count, aircraft_connect_within_24h, severity, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Insert an unauthorised USB stick on a PMAT 12 hours before that PMAT connects to an aircraft for a software load. UC-22.60.6 MUST flag within 10 minutes with risk_score >= 85.

### Negative scenario

Insert an organisation-approved USB stick (allowlisted hardware ID) on a PMAT and load an authorised LSAP. UC-22.60.6 MUST NOT flag, because the USB event matches the allowlist and the loaded LSAP signature verification (UC-22.60.4) passes.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.60.1 in production (for organisational context).
- KV Store `do355a_pmat_inventory_lookup` (PMAT asset register).
- KV Store `do355a_pmat_workorder_lookup` (work-order to tail mapping).
- Microsoft Defender Device Control or equivalent USB control on every PMAT.
- SCCM compliance baseline targeting the PMAT golden image.

## Step 1 - PMAT inventory KV Store

```ini
[do355a_pmat_inventory_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.pmat_id = string
field.serial_number = string
field.technician_id = string
field.golden_image_version = string
field.in_service = string
```

## Step 2 - Allowlist USB devices

Configure Defender Device Control with allowlist policy targeting `pmat_in_scope=true` devices: organisation-issued data-loader hardware IDs only.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.6: PMAT Security Health]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, soar
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.6,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-355A-s2-1
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 90`** (owner: CAMO Manager + CISO + Director of Maintenance, severity P1): Within 30 minutes, the PMAT is removed from the active work-order pool; the planned tails have the work-order re-issued to a known-good PMAT. The compromised PMAT is re-imaged from the golden image and re-baselined.

**`risk_score 70-89`** (owner: CAMO Manager, severity P2): Within 4 hours, the PMAT's findings are remediated; the work-order proceeds only after the PMAT is cleared.

## Step 5 - Aviation-specific notes

- **Mobile PMAT**: PMATs that travel between bases need consistent baseline enforcement; ensure SCCM compliance reaches mobile PMATs daily.
- **OEM-issued PMAT firmware**: TC-holder may issue PMAT firmware updates via CASI; ensure UC-22.60.4 verifies these as LSAPs.
- **Contractor PMATs**: line-maintenance contractors may use their own PMATs; flow-down EASA Part-IS IS.OR.235 contracting requirements to those organisations.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup do355a_pmat_inventory_lookup
| join type=outer pmat_id [
    search index=endpoint sourcetype="sccm:baseline" pmat_in_scope="true" earliest=-7d@d
    | stats latest(_time) AS last_baseline_check latest(baseline_drift_count) AS baseline_drift_finding by host AS pmat_id ]
| join type=outer pmat_id [
    search index=endpoint sourcetype="mscs:wd:atp:device-control" earliest=-24h@h
    | where device_class="USB"
    | stats count AS usb_event_count by host AS pmat_id ]
| join type=outer pmat_id [
    search index=endpoint sourcetype IN ("mscs:wd:atp:detection", "crowdstrike:falcon:detection") earliest=-24h@h
    | stats count AS malware_event_count by host AS pmat_id ]
| join type=outer pmat_id [
    | inputlookup do355a_pmat_workorder_lookup
    | where workorder_start_at > relative_time(now(), "-24h") AND aircraft_uplink_planned="true"
    | stats values(tail_number) AS planned_tails count AS aircraft_connect_within_24h by pmat_id ]
| fillnull baseline_drift_finding usb_event_count malware_event_count aircraft_connect_within_24h value=0
| eval risk_score = case(
    malware_event_count > 0 AND aircraft_connect_within_24h > 0, 100,
    malware_event_count > 0, 95,
    usb_event_count > 0 AND aircraft_connect_within_24h > 0, 90,
    baseline_drift_finding > 5 AND aircraft_connect_within_24h > 0, 85,
    baseline_drift_finding > 5, 70,
    usb_event_count > 0, 60,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| eval severity = case(risk_score >= 90, "critical", risk_score >= 70, "high", 1=1, "medium")
| sort 0 -risk_score
| table pmat_id technician_id last_baseline_check baseline_drift_finding usb_event_count malware_event_count aircraft_connect_within_24h planned_tails severity risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) PMATs at risk for next 24h aircraft uplink. (Panel 2) Malware events on PMATs (7-day trend). (Panel 3) USB events by hardware ID (allowed vs blocked). (Panel 4) Baseline-drift heatmap (PMAT x finding category).

## Known False Positives

1. **Approved USB on first use**: a newly-issued USB device may need to be added to the allowlist before its first event; allow a 4-hour grace if `device_allowlisted_pending=true`. 2. **Defender DC false positive**: certain Bluetooth peripherals on Surface Pro PMATs may register as USB devices; tune the device-class filter. 3. **Baseline drift due to authorised change**: an SCCM compliance update may briefly show drift; allow 24 hours for the next compliance scan to settle.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_6_exceptions` with the CAMO Manager + CISO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [RTCA DO-355A - Information Security Guidance for Continuing Airworthiness](https://www.rtca.org/standards/list-of-available-documents/)
- [RTCA DO-326A - Airworthiness Security Process Specification](https://www.rtca.org/standards/list-of-available-documents/)
- [EASA Part-IS Information Security Management](https://www.easa.europa.eu/en/the-agency/faqs/information-security)
